name: Geography of India: Transport & Communication; Social Geography

researchNotes: Two adjoining syllabus clauses (Transport & Communication; Social Geography — population density/distribution, racial/linguistic groups, tribes) bundled into one topic. Two direct real hits plus two web-sourced question sets that turned out to cross-label content (the 'Transport' source page actually had census/literacy facts, and the 'Social Geography' page had agriculture/ports/hydro-power facts) — used everything regardless of which page it came from, since the underlying facts are accurate and exam-relevant either way. Expanded 2026-07 pass: pulled the Kattupalli-port real hit that had been sitting unused in aptitude-evidence-stash.md since an earlier research pass, and found one more real hit via a fresh OCR scan (Q53: a genuinely counter-intuitive state-wise ranking of population aged 60+, image-verified since the OCR text alone didn't clearly show which option was checked — Bihar and Uttar Pradesh, despite huge total populations, rank LOWEST here because both are demographically young states, while Tamil Nadu ranks highest despite a smaller overall population, being one of India's most demographically 'aged' states). Rounded out Transport with standard web-sourced facts (Golden Quadrilateral, Cochin's solar-powered airport, Manali-Leh highway) and Social Geography with tribal/linguistic-diversity facts (largest tribal population in the world, 50+ recognized tribal groups, ~8.2% indigenous population share, 23 major languages covering ~97% of the population).

reading.intro: Sixth sub-topic of Geography Unit II. Real evidence shows census/literacy statistics dominate Social Geography questions, while Transport questions favor named firsts/establishment-years and true/false statement-checking.

qa.question: Real exam: What was Tamil Nadu's population density per the 2011 census?
Real exam: Rank these Tamil Nadu districts by literacy rate (highest to lowest) per the 2011 census — Nilgiris, Chennai, Thoothukudi, Kanyakumari.
What is India's overall population density (2011 census), and which state/UT has the highest?
qa.answer: India's overall density (2011) was about 382 per sq km. Among Union Territories, NCT of Delhi is the most densely populated (~11,297 per sq km); among states, Bihar ranks highest (~1,102 per sq km).

qa.question: Which delta is known as the 'Granary of South India'?
What is the second staple food of the people of Tamil Nadu (after rice)?
Name a major hydro-electric power project in Tamil Nadu, and how many major/minor ports does TN have?
qa.answer: Mettur Dam is a major hydro-electric project. Tamil Nadu has 3 major ports and 15 minor ports.

qa.question: In which year was India's first census carried out?
Which Indian state ranks first in literacy rate (93.91% per the cited figure)?
What forms the most important system of road transportation in India, and when was the National Highways Authority of India established?
qa.answer: National highways are the most important road transport system. NHAI was established in 1988.

qa.question: True or False: 'The state of Meghalaya has no railway network.'
qa.answer: FALSE — Meghalaya does have limited rail connectivity (e.g., the line to Mendipathar), even though it's one of the least rail-connected states in India.

qa.question: Real exam (2026_q page 64 Q67): '___ is declared as small port of Tiruvallur district which is closer to Ennore major port.'
qa.answer: Kattupalli (not Kulaichal, Manapaddu, or Thiruchopuram) — a minor port sitting right next to Ennore (North Chennai), often tested as a paired 'major + adjoining minor port' fact.
qa.tip: Pair them in memory: Ennore = major port, Kattupalli = its adjoining minor port, both in/near Tiruvallur district.

qa.question: Real exam (2026_q page 52 Q53): Arrange West Bengal, Andhra Pradesh, Bihar, Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu by their population aged 60-and-above (2011 census), in ASCENDING order.
qa.answer: Bihar → Uttar Pradesh → Andhra Pradesh → West Bengal → Maharashtra → Tamil Nadu (ascending). This reflects each state's stage in the demographic transition: Bihar and UP are hugely populous but demographically YOUNG (high fertility, lower median age), so their absolute elderly population is comparatively small, while Tamil Nadu and Maharashtra are among India's more demographically 'aged' states, carrying larger senior populations despite not being the most populous states outright.
qa.tip: Don't confuse TOTAL population rank with ELDERLY population rank — a state can be enormous in total population (Bihar, UP) yet rank low on the 60+ count simply because its population skews young.

qa.question: What four metro cities does the Golden Quadrilateral highway project connect, and which Indian highway is popularly cited as one of the world's highest motorable roads?
qa.answer: The Golden Quadrilateral links Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, and Kolkata via multi-lane national highways. The Manali-Leh highway (Himachal Pradesh to Ladakh) is popularly cited as one of the world's highest motorable roads, crossing several high-altitude mountain passes.

qa.question: Which Indian airport became the world's first to run entirely on solar power, and in what year?
qa.answer: Cochin International Airport, Kerala, commissioned its fully solar-powered operations in 2015 — a frequently cited example of green/sustainable transport infrastructure in India.

qa.question: Roughly what share of India's own population do its tribal/indigenous communities form, how many major recognized tribal groups exist, and how does India compare globally?
qa.answer: India has the largest tribal population in the world, with more than 50 major recognized tribal groups, together forming about 8.2% of India's total population.

qa.question: How many of India's languages are spoken by about 97% of the population, and what did the 1961 Census find about India's overall linguistic diversity?
qa.answer: 23 major languages are spoken by roughly 97% of India's population. Separately, the 1961 Census identified around 187 distinct languages in use across various sections of Indian society — a reminder of just how much linguistic diversity exists beyond the 'major' languages.

questions.prompt: Tamil Nadu's population density per the 2011 census was approximately:
Per the 2011 census, which Tamil Nadu district had the HIGHEST literacy rate among Nilgiris, Chennai, Thoothukudi, and Kanyakumari?
Which delta is known as the 'Granary of South India'?
How many major and minor ports does Tamil Nadu have respectively?
India's first census was carried out in which year?
Which Indian state has the highest literacy rate?
The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) was established in:
True or False: Meghalaya has absolutely no railway network at all.
options: False — it has limited rail connectivity
False — it has an extensive network
questions.explanation: False — Meghalaya has limited but real rail connectivity (e.g., the Mendipathar line).

questions.prompt: Real exam (2026_q Q67): Which small port of Tiruvallur district, closer to Ennore major port, is being referred to?
questions.trick: Pair them in memory: Ennore = major port, Kattupalli = its adjoining minor port.

questions.prompt: Real exam (2026_q Q53): Arrange West Bengal(1), Andhra Pradesh(2), Bihar(3), Maharashtra(4), Uttar Pradesh(5), Tamil Nadu(6) by population aged 60+ (2011 census), ascending order.
questions.explanation: Bihar(3) < Uttar Pradesh(5) < Andhra Pradesh(2) < West Bengal(1) < Maharashtra(4) < Tamil Nadu(6).
questions.trick: Total population rank and elderly (60+) population rank are NOT the same — Bihar/UP skew young, Tamil Nadu/Maharashtra skew old.

questions.prompt: The Golden Quadrilateral highway project connects which four cities?
Which Indian airport was the world's first to run entirely on solar power?
India has the largest tribal population of any country in the world, forming roughly what share of its own total population?
Roughly how many major recognized tribal groups exist in India?
questions.explanation: More than 50 major recognized tribal groups.

questions.prompt: About 97% of India's population speaks how many major languages?
Which Indian highway is popularly cited as one of the world's highest motorable roads?
questions.explanation: The Manali-Leh highway, crossing several high-altitude Himalayan passes.

questions.prompt: Among Union Territories, which is India's most densely populated (2011 census, roughly 11,297 persons per sq km)?
The 1961 Census of India recorded roughly how many distinct languages spoken across Indian society?
questions.explanation: Around 187 languages — far more than just the '23 major languages' figure, reflecting India's deep linguistic diversity.
